subject: Statistics
title: Machine Learning for Time Series
description: Learn Machine Learning for Time Series in one minute.

hook.text: Neural networks can find patterns in data that no human would spot. Can they forecast better than ARIMA?

buildup.text: LSTMs and Transformers process sequences naturally. They learn complex nonlinear patterns from raw data.

discovery.text: On some datasets, ML methods dramatically outperform traditional approaches — especially with multiple related series.

twist.text: ML models need much more data and compute. For a single short series, ARIMA often wins. ML shines at scale.

climax.text: The best modern systems combine ML flexibility with statistical interpretability. Hybrid approaches lead the field.

punchline.text: ML doesn't replace statistics for time series. It extends it.

quiz.question: When do machine learning methods outperform traditional time series approaches?
options: With large datasets and many related series where complex nonlinear patterns exist
